root: ^You're walking down a familiar path, enjoying the sounds of birds chirping and the trees rustling slightly in the wind.
^After a short while of walking, you spot a strange figure in the distance.
^Despite it being a good bit taller than you, it doesn't seem very imposing due to the way it moves.
^As you get even closer, you start to understand why this figure seems to struggle with moving.
^...You're unsure if you should see their pretentiousness as insulting or endearing. But you want to do something about this.

s: ^The apple crunches pleasantly as you bite into it.
^This might not be the time to light yourself on fire, but when has that ever stopped you?
^You decide to consult the smartest person you know, and you make some good points on how to handle this situation.
^You ponder your lockpick for a moment. Good to have one of these, you think.
^You spin the dagger in your hand, skillfully twirling it around, and around again. Finally you flip it in the air and catch it.
^You give the frying pan a spin. Cracking your head open right here would for sure alter the course of this
^You grasp your trusty staff with your dominant hand. It feels reassuring to hold.
^You quickly take a swig of the potion.
^Punching yourself would be a bit strange, even for you.
^"I'm such a failure, I don't even have a proper spellbook anymore. What kind of wizard even am I? Curse my stupid flumph life..."
^Try as you might, giving yourself a static shock proves to be impossible. Damn it.
^For a moment you consider tying yourself up but... Maybe this isn't the time. Or the place, frankly.
^You drop a smokescreen at your feet, and bolt away! You're not dealing with this
^You consider the evil potion for a moment. Surely you shouldn't drink it?
^You take out the can of soup and crack it open. Time for a little meal!
^You give the snowball a few tosses in your hand, but you can't really figure out what to do with it like this. So you give it a chomp.
^Your generous offer of an apple seems to garner the respect of the

b: ^ Better to have friends than enemies after all.
^ kobolds. This has gone too far!
^ shady figure. No one calls you chip!

^You grip the staff with both hands, and swing straight at the

b: ^Are you three stacked because you're insecure about your height? Because you're all really short."
^Your sunglasses don't fit you and the coat makes you look like a total creep!"

b: ^The kobolds seem insulted that you think you have time to snack, and they take the opportunity to attack you!
^The red kobold springs at you, its teeth sinking into
^ Pain spreads through you as you tear the creature off of you.

c-1: ^ shocked as you manifest a bonfire directly at your feet!
^The pain is immense, and the smell of burnt flesh doesn't make the experience any more pleasant.

b: ^ kobolds share a brief look among each other, and bolt away, seemingly now terrified of you!
^ figure stumbles for a moment, before apparently deciding to take its own advice, as it starts walking away.

c-2: ^ at you as if you were insane, seemingly too stunned to react.
c-4: ^ seemingly can't believe you just hit an emote on
c-5: ^But... perhaps it's better to not do that?

b: ^No. You've thought this through. Time to give in to the temptation.
^"What the hell!? Why did you do that? Are you literally insane?"
^The kobolds frantically mutter between themselves, and seem to have decided something.
^"You're crazy! We're not sticking around to find out how crazy! Bye, freak!"
^"What the hell, chip! Are you literally insane!?"
^The figure shifts strangely, then starts rapidly walking off.
^You hear the figure's final words to you as it's making its exit: "let's not meet again."
^Right. Not this time. Not yet, anyway.

c-6: ^Can't really do much with it right now, though.

b: ^You were already at full health though. At least the potion tastes somewhat sweet.
^The healing properties quickly kick in, and you regain some health.
^The healing properties quickly kick in, and you regain some health.
^ kobolds couldn't react in time to attack you!
^ kobolds seem suspicious of whatever you just drank.
^ figure couldn't really react, and it just looks slightly confused.
^The kobolds look at you with disdain and pity. You seem to have lost any respect they may have had of you.
^"Um. We're just... gonna go. Good luck with all that?"
^"Go then, everyone just leaves me anyway..."
^The kobolds are already walking away, not looking back. Victory. Of sorts, anyway.
^The kobolds look at you with disdain and pity. You seem to have lost any respect they may have had of you.
^"Great, now fighting would be stupid awkward! Stupid loser wizard!"
^"Go then, everyone just leaves me anyway..."
^The kobolds are already walking away, not looking back. Victory. Of sorts, anyway.
^The figure shifts strangely, its stoic expression failing, and turning to one of disdain.
^"Right. I'm very busy so I'll just go. Good luck with all that."
^"Go then, everyone just leaves me anyway..."
^The figure is already walking away, not looking back. Victory. Of sorts, anyway.
^You take a big swig from the bottle, and almost retch immediately. You only manage to finish it thanks to using all of your willpower.
^You feel a burn spreading through your body as the foul liquid makes its way down. This can't be good for you.
^And it isn't. You keel over, trying to make yourself smaller to somehow minimize the pain in your chest and stomach. It doesn't do much.
^A pang of empathy seems to hit the previously hostile kobolds as they share a worried look between each other.
^"Uh, you don't look so good. Maybe let's finish this fight another time."
^You try to reply, but speaking feels impossible. You give a quick, pained nod, and the kobolds start making their exit.
^You whimper on the ground for several hours, but eventually the worst of the pain passes, and you feel like you could move on.
^The kobolds share a worried look between each other, before moving slightly closer.
^"You want us to go? We should probably go right?"
^A weak "mh" is all you manage. You'd rather suffer in private.
^"Right, well. We'll go then. Good meeting you,
^You whimper on the ground for several hours, but eventually the worst of the pain passes, and you feel like you could move on.
^Speaking seems like it'd hurt a lot, so you just shake your head.
^"Thought so. I'll just keep walking, chip. Good luck with recovering from that."
^The figure does as it says, and makes its exit.
^You whimper on the ground for several hours, but eventually the worst of the pain passes, and you feel like you could move on.
^Yeah maybe not. You brewed this yourself, and you know that it'd cause significant damage to your esophagus and stomach if ingested.
^The kobolds seem insulted that you think you have time to have dinner, and they take the opportunity to attack you!

^ Pain spreads through you as you tear the creature off of you.
^ kobolds look slightly jealous, but they quickly hide their feelings.
^ figure looks on as you chomp the snowball. You wonder what its thinking.

b: ^"I accept your generous offer, chip. Good luck on your travels."
^The figure resumes its laborous walking, eventually fading into the distance
^"Wait, I thought we were fighting! Why're you giving us gifts now!" His tail is wagging.
^"I didn't mean to start a fight, hopefully the apple is enough to repay you."
^The kobolds chatter among themselves for a short while.
^"We've reached a decision! You're off the hook this time,
^. Try not to start fights with everyone you meet!"
^You wave them off as they're leaving.
^"Whoa, thanks a bunch! We'll totally try to remember this!"
^The three kobolds hop off of each other, before continuing on their way. Seems they forgot their coat.
^As you call forth a bonfire, the shady figure stumbles back a few steps.
^Seeing its uncertainty, you take out some marshmallows to hopefully pique its interest. No, wait.
^You were meant to roast the figure, not some marshmallows!
^As you ponder what exactly went wrong, the shady figure shifts around strangely.
^You hear strange bickering from inside the shady figure, which quickly ends as...
^The coat covering the figure falls off, revealing three kobolds stacked on top of each other.
^Well now that our great secret is revealed, nothing's stopping us from having the marshmallows, right?
^The red one lets out an exasperated sigh.
^You call forth a bonfire between you and the figure, taking out some marshmallows.
^You hear strange bickering from within the figure, as it shifts around strangely.
^The bickering quickly stops, as the coat covering the figure falls off, revealing a stack of three kobolds.
^Well now that our great secret is revealed, nothing's stopping us from having the marshmallows, right?
^The red one lets out an exasperated sigh.
^As you call forth a bonfire, the kobold stack stumbles back a few steps.
^Seeing their uncertainty, you take out some marshmallows to hopefully pique its interest. No, wait.
^You were meant to roast the kobolds, not some marshmallows!
^As you ponder what exactly went wrong, the white kobold voices their opinion.
^"The hat already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows it offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"The wizard lady already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows she offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"This wizard guy already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows he offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"The wizard-person already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows they offer wouldn't change anything!"
^The red one lets out an exasperated sigh.
^You call forth a bonfire between you and the kobolds, taking out some marshmallows.
^The three bicker quietly for a moment, before the white kobold voices their opinion.
^"The hat already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows it offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"The wizard lady already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows she offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"This wizard guy already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows he offers wouldn't change anything!"
^"The wizard-person already knows our great secret, taking the marshmallows they offer wouldn't change anything!"
^The red one lets out an exasperated sigh.
^"I suppose. I'm far too busy to stay for a chat, however. Keep walking, chip", the figure says while lumbering away.
^"What a weirdo", you think to yourself while watching the figure grow distant.
^You and the kobold stack both shuffle awkwardly.
^"Good talk", you say while already walking away to escape the awkwardness.
^"You too", a kobold replies before you hear a slap behind you.
^What a horribly awkward encounter. Hopefully they'll also try to forget about it.
^The lockpick is swatted away as the red kobold bites your hand!
^The red kobold gives it a confused look.
^The figure bends strangely to inspect it, before straightening back up.
^You swing at the stack of kobolds, landing a few strikes on the top one, who yelps out in pain!
^The stack quickly dismantles, and the other two rapidly carry off their wounded stack-mate.
^As the group is escaping, the dark grey one yells out: "We'll definitely finish you off next time!"
^You swing at the stack of kobolds, landing a few strikes on the top one, who yelps out in pain!
^"What the hell, lady!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, mister!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, hat!? What was that for?"
^The stack quickly dismantles, and the other two prepare carry off their wounded stack-mate.
^As the group begins their escape, the dark grey one yells out: "We'll definitely mess you up next time!"
^You swing at the figure, and while you do land some strikes, the figure seems more scared than hurt.
^You quickly find out the reason, as the figure's coat slips off, revealing a very pissed off looking stack of kobolds!
^"What the hell, lady!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, mister!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, hat!? What was that for?"
^The kobolds growl at you. You seem to have a fight on your hands.

HANDLE_BONK_OUTCOME: ^Your strike connects with the head of
^ sounding like it hurt. A lot.

b: ^A fitting punishment for calling you chip, you think.
^The figure quickly falls apart, revealing three kobolds!
^"What the hell, lady!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, mister!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, hat!? What was that for?"
^The remaining two kobolds quickly pick up their wounded stack-mate, preparing to run off.
^"Point taken, now get the hell out of the way!"
^The two kobolds still standing sprint away from you while carrying the third.
^As they make their escape, the dark grey one yells at you: "We'll definitely mess you up next time!"
^The top kobold falls from atop the stack, unconscious. You can already see a bump forming on his head.
^"What the hell, lady!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, mister!? What was that for?"
^"What the hell, hat!? What was that for?"
^The remaining two kobolds quickly pick up their wounded stack-mate, preparing to run off.
^As the kobolds start to make their escape, the dark grey one yells at you: "We'll definitely mess you up next time!"
^The top kobold falls from atop the stack, unconscious. You can already see a bump forming on his head.
^The remaining two kobolds quickly pick up their wounded stack-mate, preparing to run off.
^As the kobolds start to make their escape, the dark grey one yells at you: "We'll definitely mess you up next time!"
^"I accept your generous offer, chip", the figure says while taking the offered potion.
^"I will remember this. Now keep walking, chip"
^You seem to have garnered the respect of the figure, who is now laborously walking away from you.
^"Wow, are you sure we can take this? Thanks a lot!"
^ We'll totally try to remember this!"
^The red kobold whispers something to the others, and they nod in unison.
^The three kobolds hop off of each other, before continuing on their way. Seems they forgot their coat.
^"That's it, now you're gonna get it!"
^The red kobold springs at you, its teeth digging right into
^You manage to tear the creature off of you, but the pain remains. Time to fight back!
^"That's it, now you're gonna get it!"
^The kobolds look pretty pissed off now. It seems your insult has gotten you into a fight!
^The figure flings its coat off, revealing a stack of very pissed off kobolds!
^"We're not creeps! And the sunglasses are cool!"
^The stack starts growling at you. Seems you've gotten yourself into a fight!
^The red kobold yelps loudly, falling dramatically off from on top of the stack!
^The other two quickly de-stack as well, huddling around their injured friend. That should show them!
^The figure quickly falls apart, revealing three kobolds, one of whom seems to already be knocked out!
^The other two quickly huddle around their injured friend. That should show them?
^The dark grey one gives you a seriously hostile look.
^"What the hell, lady!? What was that for!"
^"What the hell, mister!? What was that for!"
^"What the hell, hat!? What was that for!"
^It seems to have more pressing matters than revenge right now though, as the red kobold groans on the ground.

HANDLE_SHOCK_OUTCOME: ^"Stay with us! Just focus on breathing!"
^"I... I don't think I'll make it... Please... Take care of yourselves."
^...Huh? That was a static shock, not anything seriously damaging! Is he pretending?
^Whatever the case, seems like you've won the day!
^The dark grey and white kobolds hoist their injured stackmate above their heads and start making an exit!
^"We'll definitely get you next time, you evil wizard! Definitely!"
^The kobolds skitter away, leaving you to ponder what to do next.

b: ^The kobolds try to quickly de-stack, but they're too slow!
^You quickly finish tying them up, dusting off your
^The kobolds mutter among each other, clearly peeved that they've been tied up so easily.
^"Ha, caught you! And so easily too!"
^"Well. Once you're done mocking us, could you untie us? This is really uncomfortable, especially for those two."
^"Mmm, no. You'll can repent for your actions right there."
^They groan loudly, probably realizing they can't really talk back in this position.
^"Well then, I'll be going. Have fun getting free!"
^As you try to wrap the rope around the figure, its body twists alarmingly, before falling apart entirely!
^From the coat emerge three kobolds that quickly hop back onto each other, growling at you all the while!
^"What the hell was that for!? We're totally gonna mess you up for that!"
^Seems you've landed yourself in a fight!
^"Thanks again for the marshmallows, hat-thing! Good luck with the adventuring!" The group yells, their voices growing distant.
^"Thanks again for the marshmallows, kind lady! Good luck with the adventuring!" The group yells, their voices growing distant.
^"Thanks again for the marshmallows, mister! Good luck with the adventuring!" The group yells, their voices growing distant.
^"Thanks again for the marshmallows, wizard-person! Good luck with the adventuring!" The group yells, their voices growing distant.
^What a surprisingly nice group of kobolds. Even though the red one called you chip.
^As you prepare to leave as well, you notice a few cards where the kobolds were sitting! Did they leave these for you?
^You figure they're safer in your binder than on the ground, so you gather up the intact ones.
^As you prepare to leave as well, you notice a few cards on the ground!
^You figure they're safer in your binder than on the ground, so you gather up the intact ones.
^As you start to leave, you notice a few cards on the ground!
^You figure they're safer in your binder than on the ground, so you gather up the intact ones.

KOBOLD_EVENT_EPILOGUE: ^You suppose there's nothing left to do here, and resume traveling.
